ops.title: never branch production code on "is this a test"; make tests set up real conditions via real fixtures, or don't write the test
ops.body:
Absolute rule from the user, stated sharply: **stop doing hacks to get around tests. Make tests act like real cases. Do NOT have special test logic in production. Make tests indistinguishable from real, or don't test. Remove ALL special-case-for-tests logic.**

**Why:** I proposed gating a production function (`_mcp_marker_live` → return `False` when the event's session store is null) purely so an inline `tests={}` harness would resolve deterministically. That is test-detection code masquerading as a semantic gate — the user rejected it on sight. A "branch-invariant substring" that dodges the ambient wording is the same sin from the test side (routing around the real behavior instead of controlling it).

**How to apply:** when a function reads ambient/global state (cwd's git repo, other processes' PIDs, a marker file) and that makes it non-deterministic under test, the fix is to make that input a **real, controllable input** the test establishes for real (a real session store with the real flag set, a real marker file in a real temp repo) — both branches tested as genuine cases — NOT a production branch that detects the test harness. If a test cannot be made to construct the real scenario, drop that assertion rather than pin it with a hack. Prefer redesigning the function to be a pure function of its inputs over leaving hidden global reads. Reinforces STYLEGUIDE.md "Real fixtures over mocks" / "No guards against impossible states." Related: [[cc-notes-hooks-inline-test-constraint]].
